title: The double half hitch
subtitle: Knotting rows, diagonal lines, and curves with a carrier cord
excerpt: The double half hitch (DHH) knots one working cord twice around a carrier cord held taut at an angle. A row of double half hitches along a horizontal carrier creates a solid bar; along a diagonal carrier it creates a diagonal line. Combining directions builds chevrons, curves, and geometric shapes.
sourceNotes: The double half hitch (also called the clove hitch knot row) is documented in macramé instruction texts and knotting manuals. Observation-led.

content.text: knots each working cord twice around a
held taut. The carrier can be held horizontally to produce a
, diagonally for diagonal lines, or pinned in a curve for curved rows. The carrier can be any of the existing working cords: no separate cord is needed.

steps: Hold the leftmost cord taut horizontally at the height where the bar will sit. This is the carrier cord. Pin it at the left selvedge.

Take the second cord (the first working cord). Cross it over the carrier cord, loop it under the carrier, and bring it back through the loop it has formed. Pull to tighten. This is the first half hitch.
With the same working cord, repeat: cross over the carrier, loop under, bring through the loop, and tighten. This is the second half hitch.
One double half hitch is complete: two loops of the working cord sit snugly on the carrier.
Move to the third cord and repeat both half hitches on the same carrier cord.
Continue across all working cords from left to right. Pin the carrier at the right selvedge when the row is complete.
attrs.knotName: Double half hitch (left to right row)

content.text: For a diagonal line going from top-left to bottom-right: hold the leftmost cord diagonally downward to the right as the carrier, pinning it in position at both ends. Tie double half hitches along it with each of the other cords in sequence. For a chevron, use two carriers meeting in the centre from each side.

The most common problem in DHH rows is uneven tension. The carrier must be held firmly taut at a consistent angle as each working cord knots around it. If the carrier bends or drops, the finished bar will curve. Use T-pins to pin the carrier at both ends before knotting, and keep firm downward pressure on the already-knotted section with your non-dominant hand as you tie each new knot.

attrs.intro: Two problems in double half hitch rows.

items.fix: Pin the carrier at both ends and also in the middle with a T-pin. Use a ruler or dowel as a guide behind the carrier to keep it straight.
items.cause: The carrier cord was not held taut, or it was pinned at both ends but allowed to sag between them.
items.symptom: The horizontal bar curves or dips in the middle

items.fix: After each double half hitch, use your thumbnail to push both loops up snugly against the previous knot before moving on.
items.cause: The second half hitch of each pair was not pulled up to sit directly beside the first before moving to the next working cord.
items.symptom: Knots are uneven: some sit high on the carrier, others low

attrs.heading: Common problems
